title: ECG result review

examination.text: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.
examination.rationale: The bradycardia algorithm was reviewed to confirm ECG relevance for suspected bradycardia but was not generalized to every ECG result.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

source.title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of Bradycardia and Conduction Disease Algorithm
exact_section.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF page 9, Figure 1, plus page 10 reversible-cause table

investigations.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
investigations.rationale: The NICE chest-pain information section supports documenting clinician explanation of a reviewed test, uncertainty, questions, shared decisions, safety-netting, and follow-up without generating an interpretation or advice.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice

follow_up.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.
follow_up.rationale: The NICE AF section supports documenting the ECG source and date, reason for review, related symptoms, pulse or rhythm context, and clinician interpretation where AF is suspected; it does not infer an ECG diagnosis.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source_limitations: Indication, date, technical quality, calibration, rate, rhythm, axis, intervals, waves, segments, comparison tracing, symptoms, formal interpretation, diagnosis, urgency, treatment, referral, communication, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
